chunks.text: Venkatesan Guruswami has been selected as the new director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing at UC Berkeley, following an international search. The Simons Institute is the world’s foremost center for collaborative research in theoretical computer science, bringing together scholars to tackle foundational questions about computation. The Institute has hosted more than 4,000 researchers from around the world.

Guruswami, a chancellor’s professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and professor of Mathematics at UC Berkeley, is a leading theoretical computer scientist whose research spans error correction, approximate optimization, and computational complexity. He is the Institute’s third director since its founding in 2012, succeeding Richard Karp (2012–2017) and Shafi Goldwasser (2018–2024).

“I’m honored to have this opportunity to serve the international theory community at an exciting moment,” Guruswami said. “The Simons Institute is fundamentally community-driven, and I look forward to deepening its hallmark atmosphere of immersion, collaboration, and breakthrough discovery.”

Guruswami has a distinguished record of scholarship and service, including roles as a Simons Investigator, editor in chief of the Journal of the ACM, and president of the Computational Complexity Foundation. He is a fellow of the ACM, AMS, and IEEE, and his honors include the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award, the Presburger Award, and Guggenheim, Packard, and Sloan Fellowships.
